body.content: @david-chen I am reviewing the database tables. You mentioned tenant_id filtering is strictly application-level. Are there any legacy tables missing this column entirely?

Yes. The legacy_billing_events table was created three years ago before we even conceptualized multi-tenancy. It relies on a complex join with the users and organizations tables to infer tenancy dynamically at runtime.

That is a severe liability for our migration. Drizzle's dynamic routing requires a hard tenant context to execute operations securely against the isolated schemas. If we attempt to migrate that table as-is, the data referential integrity will shatter.
I agree. I will assign an engineer to write a pre-migration script this sprint to backfill a hard tenant_id column onto those millions of legacy records.
Excellent. Please ensure the backfill script utilizes batched transactions. We do not want to lock the production database while it runs.
I'll have a junior dev pair with me on the batching logic. We'll set the batch size to 5000 rows to keep the transaction windows minimal.
Sarah, regarding the Clerk to Auth0 transition, do your Tier-1 enterprise prospects require immediate SCIM 2.0 directory synchronization, or is standard SAML SSO sufficient for the initial launch?

Three of the active deals are demanding automated SCIM provisioning. Their internal IT security policies mandate that user accounts are automatically created and revoked from their central Active Directory, rather than relying on Just-In-Time (JIT) provisioning upon first login.
Understood. I will configure the Auth0 inbound SCIM endpoints and set up the corresponding attribute mapping.

I have completed the CI/CD pipeline audit. The 35-minute build time is driven by the monolithic repository pulling down hundreds of unused dependencies during the Docker image build phase, combined with a lack of build caching.
We haven't pruned the package.json in over two years.

To achieve an immediate velocity win, I am going to implement Turborepo for workspace management. It will intelligently cache the builds and should cut deployment times in half even before we extract the microservices.
If you can get the CI/CD builds under 15 minutes, the engineering team will be ecstatic.
🎯 Finalizing Phase 1 Infrastructure Audit Document | 🚧 Waiting on Clerk API rate limit increase | ⏭️ Preparing parallel staging environments for Phase 2.
Yes, we have hit a roadblock. I am writing the Clerk bulk export scripts, but their API is rate-limiting us aggressively. We cannot extract the legacy user data fast enough.
I will escalate this to our Clerk enterprise representative immediately to have the rate limits temporarily lifted for the migration window.
Please do. We need the bulk export complete to seed the Auth0 database before we can safely activate the Trickle Migration flow.
Rate limit increase approved. They gave us a 48-hour window of unthrottled API access.
Executing the export scripts now.

The legacy_billing_events backfill script finished running in the staging environment. Data integrity checks passed. I recommend running this in production during the lowest traffic window this weekend.
David, please ensure a comprehensive rollback procedure is documented in Confluence before executing the backfill on production.
Documented, peer-reviewed, and linked in the Jira ticket.
User @chloe-kim opened Pull Request #42: feat: auth0 universal login integration

I have stripped out the localized Clerk UI components and integrated the Auth0 Next.js SDK. We are routing all authentication to the Auth0 Universal Login page to ensure we can natively enforce adaptive MFA and strict session expiration policies.
Can we ensure the Universal Login page doesn't look like a generic Auth0 prompt? It needs to maintain our brand trust.
Absolutely. I have injected your corporate CSS variables and logos into the Auth0 tenant configuration. The transition will be visually seamless for the end-user.
@channel The Phase 1 Discovery & Infrastructure Setup deliverables are officially complete and signed off. We are formally moving into Phase 2: Auth0 Migration & Security Hardening.
🎯 Coordinating SAML SSO staging tests with enterprise prospects | 🚧 None | ⏭️ Monitoring Trickle Migration success rates.
🎯 Testing Auth0 Trickle Migration logic | 🚧 None | ⏭️ Configuring dummy Okta IdP for SAML validation.
🎯 Decoupling Analytics API routes locally | 🚧 None | ⏭️ Deploying Turborepo configurations to CI/CD.
🎯 Writing Drizzle schema generation scripts | 🚧 None | ⏭️ Monitoring production database backfill stability.
The weekend database backfill was a success. No downtime, and the tenant_id column is fully populated across all legacy tables.
Great. I am initiating the SAML 2.0 integration tests using our dummy Okta enterprise account.
🚨 ALERT: Spike in 400 errors on /api/auth/callback in Staging Environment.
The SAML assertion is failing. The IdP login page displays, the user authenticates, but the Auth0 callback is rejecting the payload.
What is the exact error in the Auth0 tenant log?
I pulled the HAR file and decoded the SAML response. The error is: SAML Response invalid: Signature validation failed.
Signature validation failures almost always mean a certificate mismatch. Did the dummy Okta account rotate its x.509 signing certificate recently?
Let me check the IdP metadata... Yes. The Public Certificate stored in our Auth0 SSO configuration is outdated. I am pasting the new x.509 certificate into the Auth0 settings now.
Okay, signature validation passed. But now the callback is throwing a 403 error: Audience restriction validation failed.
Audience mismatch? What is the Service Provider (SP) Entity ID expecting?
The SAML assertion says it is meant for the audience https://staging.apexanalytics.com/metadata, but our Auth0 SP is configured to expect urn:auth0:apex-dev:okta-dummy.
Ah, that is my fault. I updated the environment variables in the staging cluster yesterday but forgot to sync the new URLs with the Auth0 Terraform provider. Pushing a fix to the IaC repository now.
User @david-chen merged Pull Request #14: fix: sync terraform auth0 environment URLs
Terraform applied. SAML SSO login is successful for the dummy account. The authentication flow is stable.
That's a huge relief. The configuration mismatch was completely preventing Auth0 from issuing the JWT tokens correctly to the client context.
Excellent troubleshooting, team. We have our first real enterprise client, 'Globex Retail', ready to test the live SAML integration in our staging environment tomorrow morning.
This is a make-or-break deal. If Globex successfully authenticates via their corporate portal, they will sign the annual procurement contract this week.
I will monitor the Datadog logs and the Auth0 tenant streams closely during their testing window.
🎯 Facilitating Globex Retail SAML test | 🚧 None | ⏭️ Preparing Phase 3 kickoff.
